Name: Annual Mean Temperature
Definition: Mean of the 12 monthly mean temperatures

Name: Mean Diurnal Range
Definition: Mean of monthly (max temp - min temp)

Name: Isothermality
Definition: bio2 / bio7

Name: Temperature Seasonality
Definition: Standard deviation of monthly mean temperatures

Name: Max Temperature of Warmest Month
Definition: Highest monthly maximum temperature

Name: Min Temperature of Coldest Month
Definition: Lowest monthly minimum temperature

Name: Temperature Annual Range
Definition: bio5 - bio6

Name: Mean Temperature of Wettest Quarter
Definition: Mean temperature of the wettest 3 consecutive months

Name: Mean Temperature of Driest Quarter
Definition: Mean temperature of the driest 3 consecutive months

Name: Mean Temperature of Warmest Quarter
Definition: Mean temperature of the warmest 3 consecutive months

Name: Mean Temperature of Coldest Quarter
Definition: Mean temperature of the coldest 3 consecutive months

Name: Annual Precipitation
Definition: Sum of the 12 monthly precipitation totals
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Precipitation of Wettest Month
Definition: Precipitation of the month with the highest total
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Precipitation of Driest Month
Definition: Precipitation of the month with the lowest total
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Precipitation Seasonality
Definition: Coefficient of variation of monthly precipitation

Name: Precipitation of Wettest Quarter
Definition: Total precipitation of the wettest 3 consecutive months
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Precipitation of Driest Quarter
Definition: Total precipitation of the driest 3 consecutive months
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Precipitation of Warmest Quarter
Definition: Total precipitation of the warmest 3 consecutive months
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Precipitation of Coldest Quarter
Definition: Total precipitation of the coldest 3 consecutive months
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Maximum monthly climate moisture index
Definition: Climate moisture index of the month with the largest precipitation surplus
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Mean monthly climate moisture index
Definition: Average monthly climate moisture index over one year
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Minimum monthly climate moisture index
Definition: Climate moisture index of the month with the largest precipitation deficit
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Annual range of monthly climate moisture index
Definition: Difference between maximum and minimum monthly climate moisture index
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Maximum monthly total cloud cover
Definition: Highest monthly total cloud cover

Name: Mean monthly total cloud cover
Definition: Average monthly total cloud cover over one year

Name: Minimum monthly total cloud cover
Definition: Lowest monthly total cloud cover

Name: Annual range of monthly total cloud cover
Definition: Difference between maximum and minimum monthly total cloud cover

Name: Maximum monthly near-surface relative humidity
Definition: Highest monthly near-surface relative humidity

Name: Mean monthly near-surface relative humidity
Definition: Average monthly near-surface relative humidity over one year

Name: Minimum monthly near-surface relative humidity
Definition: Lowest monthly near-surface relative humidity

Name: Annual range of monthly near-surface relative humidity
Definition: Difference between maximum and minimum near-surface relative humidity

Name: Maximum monthly vapour pressure deficit
Definition: Highest monthly vapour pressure deficit

Name: Mean monthly vapour pressure deficit
Definition: Average monthly vapour pressure deficit over one year

Name: Minimum monthly vapour pressure deficit
Definition: Lowest monthly vapour pressure deficit

Name: Annual range of monthly vapour pressure deficit
Definition: Difference between maximum and minimum monthly vapour pressure deficit

Name: Maximum monthly potential evapotranspiration
Definition: Highest monthly PET (Penman-Monteith)
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Mean monthly potential evapotranspiration
Definition: Average monthly PET over one year (Penman-Monteith)
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Minimum monthly potential evapotranspiration
Definition: Lowest monthly PET (Penman-Monteith)
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Annual range of monthly potential evapotranspiration
Definition: Difference between maximum and minimum monthly PET
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Maximum monthly surface downwelling shortwave flux
Definition: Highest monthly surface downwelling shortwave flux in air
Notes: confirmed 404 at CHELSA's server as of 2026-07-24 — not currently fetchable regardless of unit

Name: Mean monthly surface downwelling shortwave flux
Definition: Average monthly surface downwelling shortwave flux over one year
Notes: confirmed 404 at CHELSA's server as of 2026-07-24 — not currently fetchable regardless of unit

Name: Minimum monthly surface downwelling shortwave flux
Definition: Lowest monthly surface downwelling shortwave flux in air
Notes: confirmed 404 at CHELSA's server as of 2026-07-24 — not currently fetchable regardless of unit

Name: Annual range of monthly surface downwelling shortwave flux
Definition: Difference between maximum and minimum monthly shortwave flux
Notes: confirmed 404 at CHELSA's server as of 2026-07-24 — not currently fetchable regardless of unit

Name: Maximum monthly near-surface wind speed
Definition: Highest monthly near-surface wind speed

Name: Mean monthly near-surface wind speed
Definition: Average monthly near-surface wind speed over one year

Name: Minimum monthly near-surface wind speed
Definition: Lowest monthly near-surface wind speed

Name: Annual range of monthly near-surface wind speed
Definition: Difference between maximum and minimum monthly near-surface wind speed

Name: Frost change frequency
Definition: Number of events in which tmin or tmax crosses 0 C

Name: First day of the growing season (TREELIM)
Definition: First day of the growing season under the TREELIM model

Name: Last day of the growing season (TREELIM)
Definition: Last day of the growing season under the TREELIM model

Name: Growing season length (TREELIM)
Definition: Length of the growing season

Name: Growing season precipitation (TREELIM)
Definition: Precipitation accumulated over all growing season days
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); gsl is not a fixed physical time unit, so no conversion to it is attempted — repo treats gsp as a plain total, confirmed by downloading gsl+precipitation and checking practical bounds

Name: Growing season mean temperature (TREELIM)
Definition: Mean temperature of all growing season days

Name: Growing degree days above 0 C
Definition: Heat sum of all days above 0 C accumulated over one year

Name: Growing degree days above 5 C
Definition: Heat sum of all days above 5 C accumulated over one year

Name: Growing degree days above 10 C
Definition: Heat sum of all days above 10 C accumulated over one year

Name: First growing degree day above 0 C
Definition: First day of the year above 0 C

Name: First growing degree day above 5 C
Definition: First day of the year above 5 C

Name: First growing degree day above 10 C
Definition: First day of the year above 10 C

Name: Last growing degree day above 0 C
Definition: Last day of the year above 0 C

Name: Last growing degree day above 5 C
Definition: Last day of the year above 5 C

Name: Last growing degree day above 10 C
Definition: Last day of the year above 10 C

Name: Number of growing degree days above 0 C
Definition: Number of days on which tas > 0 C

Name: Number of growing degree days above 5 C
Definition: Number of days on which tas > 5 C

Name: Number of growing degree days above 10 C
Definition: Number of days on which tas > 10 C

Name: Snow cover days
Definition: Number of days with snow cover (TREELIM snowpack implementation)

Name: Net primary productivity
Definition: Potential NPP calculated with the Miami model (Lieth 1972)

Name: Snow water equivalent
Definition: Amount of liquid water if the snow were melted
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Koppen-Geiger climate classification
Definition: Koppen-Geiger (Koeppen & Geiger 1936); 31 classes

Name: Koppen-Geiger climate classification
Definition: Koppen-Geiger without As/Aw differentiation

Name: Koppen-Geiger climate classification
Definition: Koppen-Geiger after Peel et al. 2007

Name: Wissmann climate classification
Definition: Wissmann 1939; 22 classes

Name: Thornthwaite climate classification
Definition: Thornthwaite 1931; 30 classes

Name: Troll-Pfaffen climate classification
Definition: Troll-Pfaffen (Troll & Paffen 1964); 37 classes

Name: Site water balance
Definition: Cumulative water available through the year; capped by soil available water holding capacity
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow
